2605.27932.title: When Think-with-Image Meets Safety: What Determines Multimodal Jailbreak Robustness?
2605.27932.paper_summary: Think-with-image reasoning is emerging as a new inference paradigm for large vision-language models, but its safety implications remain poorly understood. Existing systems already span multiple process designs, including direct response generation, text-only prior turn, visual-state manipulation, and explicit external image-tool invocation. In this paper, we ask which of these evaluated paradigms improves multimodal jailbreak robustness, and why. Across multiple vision-language models, explicit image-tool interaction yields the lowest attack success rates in our experiments, reducing jailbreak success by around 30% relative on average across the evaluated models. This finding is initially surprising: ASR remains low even when the returned image-tool output is manually overridden or itself unsafe-looking, but returns near direct-answering levels under text-only prior turn controls. These results indicate that the lower ASR is not explained by benign returned-image semantics or by the textual image-tool trace alone. To explain the pattern, we introduce an image-tool safety vector framework that models image-tool invocation as a residual shift in hidden representations toward a safety-relevant direction. Representation-level analyses and activation interventions support this account. Overall, our results suggest that explicit image-tool interaction is a promising design pattern for improving jailbreak robustness, while also motivating pipeline-specific safety evaluation.
2605.27932.paper_summary_zh: 思維影像推理正在成為大型視覺-語言模型的一種新推理範式，但其安全性影響仍然不甚了解。現有系統已涵蓋多種過程設計，包括直接回應生成、僅文本的前一輪、視覺狀態操作以及明確的外部影像工具調用。在本文中，我們探討這些評估的範式中，哪一種能提高多模態越獄的穩健性，以及原因為何。在多個視覺-語言模型中，明確的影像工具互動在我們的實驗中產生了最低的攻擊成功率，平均減少約30%的越獄成功率。這一發現最初令人驚訝：即使返回的影像工具輸出被手動覆蓋或本身看起來不安全，ASR仍然保持在低水平，但在僅文本的前一輪控制下接近直接回答的水平。這些結果表明，較低的ASR並不是由於返回影像的良性語義或僅由文本影像工具痕跡所解釋。為了解釋這一模式，我們引入了一個影像工具安全向量框架，將影像工具的調用建模為隱藏表示向安全相關方向的殘差轉移。表示層級的分析和激活介入支持這一解釋。總的來說，我們的結果表明，明確的影像工具互動是一種有前景的設計模式，可以提高越獄的穩健性，同時也促進了特定流程的安全評估。
2605.27932.author: Yuan Tian et.al.
2605.27932.authors: Yuan Tian, Bing Hu, Fang Wu, Xiaomin Li, Binghang Lu, Neil Zhenqiang Gong
